title: Maximum Value of an Ordered Triplet I
titleCn: 有序三元组中的最大值 I

testcases.input: nums = [12,6,1,2,7] Output: 77 Explanation: The value of the triplet (0, 2, 4) is (nums[0] - nums[2]) * nums[4] = 77. It can be shown that there are no ordered triplets of indices with a value greater than 77.
testcases.expectedOutput: 77 Explanation: The value of the triplet (0, 2, 4) is (nums[0] - nums[2]) * nums[4] = 77. It can be shown that there are no ordered triplets of indices with a value greater than 77.

testcases.input: nums = [1,10,3,4,19] Output: 133 Explanation: The value of the triplet (1, 2, 4) is (nums[1] - nums[2]) * nums[4] = 133. It can be shown that there are no ordered triplets of indices with a value greater than 133.
testcases.expectedOutput: 133 Explanation: The value of the triplet (1, 2, 4) is (nums[1] - nums[2]) * nums[4] = 133. It can be shown that there are no ordered triplets of indices with a value greater than 133.

testcases.input: nums = [1,2,3] Output: 0 Explanation: The only ordered triplet of indices (0, 1, 2) has a negative value of (nums[0] - nums[1]) * nums[2] = -3. Hence, the answer would be 0.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: The only ordered triplet of indices (0, 1, 2) has a negative value of (nums[0] - nums[1]) * nums[2] = -3. Hence, the answer would be 0.
